etymology.description: semantic 犭(犬) (quǎn, "dog") + phonetic 侯(侯) (hóu) -> monkey
etymology.interpretation: The character 猴(hóu, "monkey") combines 犭(犬) (quǎn, "dog"), which represents animals, with 侯(侯) (hóu), serving as the phonetic component to suggest the sound. The combination naturally denotes an animal resembling a monkey.
etymology.simplification: 猴 → 猴: remains unchanged in simplification.

examples.chinese: 猴子喜欢吃香蕉(猴子喜歡吃香蕉)。
山上的猴子很顽皮(山上的猴子很頑皮)。
examples.pinyin: Shān shàng de hóuzi hěn wánpí.
examples.english: The monkeys on the mountain are very mischievous.

examples.chinese: 他像个猴子一样灵活(他像個猴子一樣靈活)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xiàng gè hóuzi yīyàng línghuó.
examples.english: He is as agile as a monkey.
